channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Escalations Damage Hormuz Media Narrative
item.description: Escalations over the past week have damaged the media narrative that has built up over the past few weeks, Standard Chartered Bank Energy Research Head Emily Ashford told Rigzone.

item.title: Chesapeake Utilities unveils $1.2bn Florida Energy Pathway plan
item.description: Chesapeake Utilities, along with its subsidiary, Peninsula Pipeline, has announced plans for the $1.2bn Florida Energy Pathway, an intrastate natural gas infrastructure project in south Florida, US.

item.title: Halliburton to deliver drilling services at Suriname’s GranMorgu field
item.description: Halliburton has been awarded contracts to provide integrated drilling and completions services for the GranMorgu deepwater oil development located around 150km off Suriname’s coast.

item.title: Cross-Industry Group Issues Letter to Incoming UK Gov
item.description: A 'powerful' cross-industry group is delivering a 'strongly worded letter' to the incoming UK government, a statement sent to Rigzone by OEUK recently noted.

item.title: BP Flags $1B Writedown from Low Carbon Business
item.description: BP said it expects to write down another $1 billion from energy transition assets in the second quarter.

item.title: Houthis Strike Saudi Arabia in Major Escalation
item.description: The Iran-backed Houthi group in Yemen fired ballistic missiles and drones on Saudi Arabia.

item.title: SLB OneSubsea wins EPC contract for Baleine phase three
item.description: SLB OneSubsea has secured an engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) contract from Eni for Phase 3 of the Baleine project located offshore Côte d’Ivoire.

item.title: Asian Oil Importers Pivot to U.S. Supplies as Hormuz Risks Intensify
item.description: Asian buyers are back to negotiating spot cargoes of U.S. crude oil after the expected recovery of flows through the Strait of Hormuz ground to a halt early this week with the latest re-escalation in the U.S.-Iran…

item.title: China Cuts Saudi Crude Orders as Hormuz Risks and Discounts Reshape Trade
item.description: Some Chinese refiners have not nominated term crude cargoes from Saudi Arabia for August, while others have not been allocated any term supply for next month, as weak demand in China, competition from other producers…

item.title: Brent Surges Above $86 After Iran Strikes Two UAE Tankers in Hormuz
item.description: Brent Crude prices soared by 3% early on Tuesday to above $86 per barrel, following an Iranian attack on two tankers of the United Arab Emirates in the southern lane of the Strait of Hormuz, in one of the biggest…

item.title: Iran Sneaks 12 Million Barrels Past Renewed U.S. Oil Blockade
item.description: Iran is estimated to have sneaked supertankers with a total of 12 million barrels of crude oil in the week between the end of the U.S. waiver on Iranian oil sales on July 7 and July 14, just as the U.S. announced the…

item.title: China’s Crude Oil Imports Crash to Decade Low as Hormuz Crisis Bites
item.description: China’s crude oil imports crashed to a decade-low in June as the reduced flows through the Strait of Hormuz hiked oil prices and reduced refiners’ appetite for costly crude. Overall Chinese imports of crude oil plunged…

item.title: Oil Prices Have Jumped 12% Since Friday as War Risks Return
item.description: Oil prices extended Monday’s surge into Asian trade on Tuesday, with Brent Crude prices hitting a one-month high, as the renewed war risk premium crashed the calmer trade of the past weeks amid renewed U.S.-Iran…

item.title: Oil Soars on Hormuz Blockade
item.description: Oil surged after President Trump announced plans to reinstate a blockade on Iranian shipping and impose fees on cargo moving through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Oil Refiners Are Cashing In on a Market That Won’t Stay Broken
item.description: Oil refiners have stumbled into one of the best profit environments in years. Crude prices have fallen back to where they traded before the Iran war erupted. But gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel remain stubbornly…

item.title: America Extends Its Lead Over Russia and Saudi Arabia in Oil Output
item.description: While the United States has been the world’s biggest consumer of oil for decades, it became the largest oil producer, too, back in 2018, and has retained that crown ever since. The shale revolution and the growth in the…

item.title: OPEC Cuts Demand Forecast Again as the Oil Market Starts Looking Past Hormuz
item.description: OPEC has trimmed its 2026 global oil demand growth forecast for the third straight month, even as crude production rebounds across the Gulf and tanker traffic slowly returns to the Strait of Hormuz. In its monthly oil…

item.title: Why the UAE Is Taking a Bigger Piece of America’s LNG Crown Jewel
item.description: As might be expected in the zero-sum game of global energy, there is much more than meets the eye to the acquisition by Abu Dhabi-headquartered international investment company XRG of an additional equity interest in…

item.title: Two Geothermal Bets Are Starting to Undercut Nuclear on Cost
item.description: While the Trump administration keeps pushing domestic energy addition through funding vehicles and executive orders aimed at grid reliability and AI-driven demand growth, two next-generation geothermal developers just…

item.title: Russian Refinery Runs Plunge to Lowest in More Than 2 Decades
item.description: A wave of Ukrainian attacks has pushed Russian refining runs to the lowest in more than 21 years.
